title: [Bug]: Almost all packets are lost when create macvlan on balance-alb bond interface using netavark network stack
body:
### Issue Description

Almost all packets are lost when create macvlan on balance-alb bond interface using netavark network stack

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create balance-alb bond on interface1 and interface2
2. Make sure it use netavark as backend network 
3. Run "podman network create" to create macvlan on bond interface as following:
podman network create -d macvlan --subnet=[subnet] -o parent=[bond-name] -o mode=bridge --gateway=[gateway][bond-name]
4. Run "podman run " to create container on the network which was created in steps 2 as following:
podman run -itd --name=test --network=[bond-name] --ip=[ipv4-address] [image-id] /bin/bash
5. Ping the container


### Describe the results you received

The container cannot be ping. I capture the packets and found that the replied MAC are from parent ethernet, not from container.

### Describe the results you expected

The container can be ping, the replied packets should be from container.

comments.body:
@susanatgit podman info says you use cni
>networkBackend: cni

Regardless I don't think there is anything netavark or CNI is doing that would cause it. Sounds to me like a kernel problem.
I suggest you create a macvlan device and netns with `ip ...` to test.

@Luap99 ,
It can be ping after I create a macvlan link on bond interface balance-alb.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

There is nothing special in netavark/CNI to handle this, if this does not work it means macvlan is not compatible on a bonded device. A quick search results in this kernel thread so I believe it is in fact a kernel problem: https://lore.kernel.org/netdev/1272.1687398130@famine/T/#m5e520b8fe877c7647dd6363633ee1341344640e5

How did you test this with ip addr? Please provide the full commands.
